paper_title: Palynoindication of the environment in the impact zone of the Apatit mining processing plant with the use of pollen of Pinus sylvestris L.

methods.method_name: Study Site Selection and Sample Collection
methods.description: Process for selecting study sites based on proximity to an industrial tailing dump and a control location, and subsequently collecting male cones (microstrobils) from Pinus sylvestris trees at these sites for pollen analysis.

steps.description: The studies were carried out in the vicinity of the apatite-nepheline tailing dump located 7 km from the ANOF-2 industrial site on the shore of the Belaya Bay of the Lake Imandra [11]. In May 2020, in the areas where fragments of taiga were preserved, test plots were laid: P 1 -2 km from the center of the tailing dump of the ANOF-2 concentrating mill in the southeast direction: P 2 -Kozlova Str., on the outskirts of the city of Apatity, near the concentrating mill, 9.5 km from the center of the tailing dump. The control site is located in the Verkhnetulomsky settlement, 122.8 km to the north-west.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and define study sites including test plots near a tailing dump and a distant control site.
inputs: Geographical location information (tailing dump, industrial site, city, settlement)

steps.description: At the end of June, 7 microstrobils with matured pollen were collected from each of five P. sylvestris trees at all sites (N = 35).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect male cones (microstrobils) from a specified number of Pinus sylvestris trees at each defined study site.

methods.description: Procedures for drying, fixing, storing, and staining collected microstrobils and pollen grains in preparation for microscopic analysis.

steps.searchable_summary: Fix dried male cones using 40% alcohol solution.

steps.description: The collected material was stored in a refrigerator at 0-4° С.
steps.searchable_summary: Store fixed male cones in a refrigerator at 0-4°C.

steps.description: For pollen analysis, microsporophylls from the middle part of microstrobil were used...
steps.searchable_summary: Select microsporophylls from the middle section of the microstrobil for pollen extraction.
Stain pollen extracted from microsporophylls using Lugol's solution.

methods.description: Detailed procedure for examining stained pollen samples under a light microscope, including preparation of slides, observation, counting, and photographing pollen grains.

steps.description: From each sample, 10 micro preparations of stained pollen were studied.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare 10 microscope slides (micropreparations) from each stained pollen sample.

steps.description: The studies were carried out using light microscopy with a magnification of 200 times.
steps.searchable_summary: Examine micropreparations using light microscopy at 200x magnification.

steps.description: Pollen analysis was carried out on the basis of micrographs taken through the eyepiece of a microscope with a 13 megapixel Xiaomi Redmi Note 5A Prime camera.
steps.searchable_summary: Capture images (micrographs) of pollen grains through the microscope eyepiece using a camera.

steps.description: On the basis of micrographs of each micropreparation (several tens of light fields), 50 pollen grains were described from photographs of good quality.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze micrographs to describe morphological characteristics of 50 pollen grains per micropreparation, selecting images of good quality.

steps.description: In each sample, 500 pollen grains were analyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: Count and classify a total of 500 pollen grains per sample (aggregated from multiple micropreparations/fields of view).
inputs: Morphological descriptions/observations from multiple fields of view
outputs: Count of different pollen types (e.g., normally developed, abnormal) per sample

methods.description: Method for assessing the level of environmental pollution at study sites based on the percentage of normally developed Pinus sylvestris pollen grains, using a specific ecological zoning scale developed by N. A. Kalashnik.

steps.description: Palynoindication of the environment was carried out based on the content of normally developed pine pollen in the samples after N. A. Kalashnik [7].
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the percentage of normally developed pollen grains in each sample.
inputs: Count of normally developed pollen grains (from Microscopic Pollen Analysis)
Total count of pollen grains analyzed per sample (500)
outputs: Percentage of normally developed pollen per sample

steps.description: According to this scale of ecological zoning of territories, pollen samples of conditionally clean territories contain more than 90% of normally developed pollen, moderately polluted -89.4-82.9%, heavily polluted -82.3 -75.2%, critically polluted -68.6-62%.
steps.searchable_summary: Classify each sample's site into an ecological zoning category based on the calculated percentage of normally developed pollen and the provided scale thresholds.
inputs: Percentage of normally developed pollen per sample
N. A. Kalashnik ecological zoning scale thresholds
outputs: Ecological zoning category for each study site (e.g., conditionally clean, moderately polluted)
